relius_title: Qualified Automatic Contribution Safe Harbor Account
relius_provision_text: 'Qualified Automatic Contribution Safe Harbor Account' means the account established hereunder to which Qualified Automatic Contribution Safe Harbor Contributions are allocated. Amounts in the Qualified Automatic Contribution Safe Harbor Account are subject to the distribution restrictions of Section 12.2(e). The Administrator may maintain a separate Qualified Automatic Contribution Safe Harbor Account for matching contributions and nonelective contributions made pursuant to a QACA. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means any Basic Matching Contributions, Enhanced Matching Contributions, and Safe Harbor Nonelective Contributions under either the Safe Harbor CODA provisions or the QACA provisions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern matching contributions under a Qualified Automatic Contribution Arrangement (QACA) and include options for employer contributions based on employee elective deferrals. They both outline the structure for contributions and eligibility requirements, indicating a similar intent and function.
variance_notes: The Relius provision specifically mentions the Qualified Automatic Contribution Safe Harbor Account and its distribution restrictions, while the Ascensus provision encompasses a broader category of contributions, including Basic and Enhanced Matching Contributions. The specific options for matching contributions differ slightly in wording but align in purpose.

relius_provision_text: 'In-Plan Roth Rollover Account' means the account attributable to an 'in-Plan Roth rollover contribution' (an IRR) that is directly rolled over within this Plan, as defined and described in Section 12.11. The amount thus contributed retains the characteristics of the source Account from which the amount of the IRR was distributed (except for the tax treatment of such amount when distributed out of the Plan). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means any contribution made either as a Direct In-Plan Roth Rollover or an Indirect In-Plan Roth Rollover. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to in-plan Roth rollover contributions, but the Relius provision specifies the characteristics of the account and the source of the rollover, while the Ascensus provision does not provide such detail and includes both direct and indirect rollovers without further elaboration.
variance_notes: The Relius provision emphasizes the retention of characteristics from the source account and specifies the nature of the rollover, whereas the Ascensus provision is broader and does not mention the characteristics of the source account or the tax treatment upon distribution.

relius_provision_text: 'Qualified Matching Contribution Account' means the account established hereunder to which Qualified Matching Contributions are allocated. Amounts in the Qualified Matching Contributions Account are nonforfeitable when made and are subject to the distribution restrictions of Section 12.2(e). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions that are nonforfeitable when allocated to the Participants' Individual Accounts and that are distributable only in accordance with the distribution provisions (other than for hardships) applicable to Elective Deferrals. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the same plan feature of Qualified Matching Contributions, indicating that contributions are nonforfeitable when allocated and subject to specific distribution restrictions. The options available in both templates relate to matching contributions, although the Ascensus template includes additional eligibility requirements.
variance_notes: The Relius provision explicitly mentions the distribution restrictions of Section 12.2(e), while the Ascensus provision refers to distribution provisions applicable to Elective Deferrals without specifying hardship exceptions. Additionally, the Relius template includes specific matching contribution options, while the Ascensus template focuses on eligibility requirements and QACA contributions.

relius_provision_text: 'Qualified Nonelective Contribution Account' means the account established hereunder to which Qualified Nonelective Contributions are allocated. Amounts in the Qualified Nonelective Contribution Account are nonforfeitable when made and are subject to the distribution restrictions of Section 12.2(e). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Employer Contributions (other than Matching Contributions, Qualified Matching Contributions, or Employer Profit Sharing Contributions) allocated to Participants' Individual Accounts that the Participants may not elect to receive in cash until distributed from the Plan; that are nonforfeitable when allocated to the Participants' Individual Accounts; and that are distributable only in accordance with the distribution provisions (other than hardships) that are applicable to Elective Deferrals. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions, specifically Qualified Nonelective Contributions, but they differ in their definitions and the context of their application. The Relius provision focuses on the allocation of contributions and their nonforfeitable nature, while the Ascensus provision emphasizes the restrictions on distribution and eligibility for contributions. Additionally, the related adoption agreement elections differ significantly in structure and options available.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific matching contribution options and mentions safe harbor contributions, while the Ascensus provision includes eligibility requirements and a QACA basic matching contribution option, indicating a broader focus on participant eligibility and contribution types.

relius_provision_text: 'Qualified Voluntary Employee Contribution Account' means the account established hereunder to which a Participant's tax-deductible qualified voluntary Employee contributions made pursuant to Section 4.9 are allocated. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: For all purposes under the Plan other than eligibility, contribution, allocation, and vesting determinations (e.g., testing and distribution eligibility), Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions will be designated as follows. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions, but they focus on different aspects. The Relius provision specifically defines a Qualified Voluntary Employee Contribution Account and discusses tax-deductible contributions, while the Ascensus provision addresses Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions without defining a specific account type. The options available in the adoption agreements also differ significantly, with Relius focusing on employer nonelective contributions and Ascensus on eligibility and allocation formulas.
variance_notes: Key differences include the specific focus on voluntary contributions in Relius versus prevailing wage contributions in Ascensus, and the lack of a defined account structure in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: 'Combined Account' means the account representing the Participant's total interest under the Plan resulting from (1) the Employer's contributions in the case of a Profit Sharing Plan or Money Purchase Plan, and (2) the Employer Non-elective Contributions in the case of a 401(k) Profit Sharing Plan. In addition, Forfeitures are part of the Combined Account to the extent they are reallocated. Separate accounting shall be maintained with respect to that portion of a Participant's Account attributable to Employer contributions made pursuant to Section 12.1(a)(2) and to Employer contributions made pursuant to Section 12.1(a)(3). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_title: ACP TEST SAFE HARBOR MATCHING CONTRIBUTIONS
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions described in Plan Section 3.01(F) or Plan Section 3.03(B). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions under retirement plans, but they govern different aspects and have different structures. The Relius provision focuses on the Combined Account and includes specific details about employer contributions and forfeitures, while the Ascensus provision references ACP test safe harbor matching contributions without detailing the same level of specificity regarding contributions. Additionally, the available options in the adoption agreements differ significantly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific contributions types and conditions, while the Ascensus provision is more general and focuses on eligibility and basic matching contributions under QACA. The Relius provision also includes details about forfeitures and separate accounting, which are not addressed in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: 'Rollover Account' means the account established hereunder to which amounts transferred from a qualified plan (including this Plan) or individual retirement account in accordance with Section 4.7 are allocated. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The value of the Roth IRA for purposes of this Plan Section 3.15 is the prior December 31 balance adjusted to include the amount of any outstanding rollovers, transfers, and recharacterizations under Q&As 7 and 8 of Treasury Regulation section 1.408-8. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to rollover accounts and distributions, but they govern different aspects of rollover contributions. The Relius provision focuses on the establishment of a Rollover Account and its relation to required minimum distributions, while the Ascensus provision discusses the value of a Roth IRA and includes specific references to outstanding rollovers and transfers. The options available in the adoption agreements also differ in focus, with Relius emphasizing hardship distributions and minimum distribution requirements, whereas Ascensus provides options for requesting distributions and accepting rollover contributions.
variance_notes: Key differences include the focus on required minimum distributions in Relius and the specific treatment of Roth IRAs in Ascensus. The options for distributions and rollover contributions are structured differently, indicating that they do not produce the same plan outcome.

relius_provision_text: 'Elective Deferral Account' means the account established hereunder to which Elective Deferrals (including a separate accounting for Catch-Up Contributions) are allocated. Amounts in the Participant's Elective Deferral Account are nonforfeitable when made and are subject to the distribution restrictions of Section 12.2(e). The Elective Deferral Account may consist of the following sub-Accounts: (1) 'Pre-Tax Elective Deferral Account' means the portion of the Elective Deferral Account attributable to Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals (i.e., Elective Deferrals that are not subject to federal income tax at the time of their deferral to the Plan). (2) 'Roth Elective Deferral Account' means the portion of the Elective Deferral Account attributable to Roth Elective Deferrals (i.e., that are subject to federal income tax at the time of their deferral to the Plan). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Excess Contributions allocated to a Participant will be distributed from the Participant's Elective Deferral account(s) and Qualified Matching Contribution account (if applicable) in proportion to the Participant's Elective Deferrals and Qualified Matching Contributions (to the extent used in the ADP test) for the Plan Year. The Plan Administrator, in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, will determine whether the distribution of Excess Contributions for a year will be made first from the Participant's Pre-Tax Elective Deferral account or the Roth Elective Deferral account, or a combination of both, to the extent both Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals and Roth Elective Deferrals were made for the year, or may allow Participants to specify otherwise. Excess Contributions will be distributed from the Participant's Qualified Nonelective Contribution account to the extent such Excess Contributions exceed the balance in the Participant's Elective Deferral accounts. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to the handling of contributions and distributions within a retirement plan, specifically focusing on Elective Deferrals and matching contributions. However, the Relius provision primarily defines the Elective Deferral Account and its components, while the Ascensus provision addresses the distribution of Excess Contributions from these accounts. The focus and context differ significantly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision does not address Excess Contributions or their distribution, which is a key aspect of the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Relius provision includes specific definitions for Pre-Tax and Roth accounts, while the Ascensus provision discusses the distribution process without defining these terms.

relius_provision_text: 'In-Plan Roth Transfer Account' means the account attributable to an in-Plan Roth rollover transfer (an IRT) that is directly rolled over within this Plan, as defined and described in Section 12.11. The amount thus contributed retains the characteristics of the source Account from which the amount of the IRT was distributed (except for the tax treatment of such amount when distributed out of the Plan). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The value of the Roth IRA for purposes of this Plan Section 3.15 is the prior December 31 balance adjusted to include the amount of any outstanding rollovers, transfers, and recharacterizations under Q&As 7 and 8 of Treasury Regulation section 1.408-8. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to Roth accounts and distributions, but they govern different aspects of Roth transfers and rollovers. The Relius provision focuses on in-plan Roth transfers and their characteristics, while the Ascensus provision addresses the valuation of Roth IRAs and the acceptance of rollover contributions.
variance_notes: The Relius provision specifically defines an In-Plan Roth Transfer Account and its characteristics, while the Ascensus provision discusses the valuation of Roth IRAs and the acceptance of rollover contributions from IRAs, indicating a broader scope. Additionally, the adoption agreement elections differ in focus, with Relius emphasizing minimum distributions and hardship distributions, whereas Ascensus focuses on the timing and acceptance of rollover contributions.

relius_provision_text: "ACP" means the "Actual Contribution Percentage" determined pursuant to Section 12.6(d). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means the average of the Contribution Percentages of the Eligible Participants in a group of either Highly Compensated Employees or non-Highly Compensated Employees. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to the Actual Contribution Percentage (ACP) but define it differently. The Relius provision focuses on the determination method, while the Ascensus provision describes the average of Contribution Percentages among different employee groups. The related adoption agreement elections also differ significantly, with Relius focusing on employer nonelective contributions and Ascensus on eligibility and allocation formulas.
variance_notes: Key differences include the definition of ACP and the related elections. Relius emphasizes employer contributions, while Ascensus addresses participant eligibility and allocation methods.

relius_provision_text: "Act" means the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, as it may be amended from time to time and includes applicable Department of Labor (DOL) guidance.
ascensus_provision_text: Means the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 as amended from time to time.
reasoning: Both provisions define the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) and indicate that it may be amended over time. The Ascensus provision is slightly less detailed as it does not mention the inclusion of applicable Department of Labor (DOL) guidance, but this does not affect the fundamental meaning.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes a reference to DOL guidance, which may imply additional compliance considerations not explicitly stated in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: "ADP" means the "Actual Deferral Percentage" determined pursuant to Section 12.4(d). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, for a specified group of Participants (either Highly Compensated Employees or non-Highly Compensated Employees) for a Plan Year, the average of the ratios (calculated separately for each Participant in such group) of 1) the amount of Employer Contributions actually paid to the Fund on behalf of such Participant for the Plan Year to 2) the Participant’s Compensation for such Plan Year. For purposes of calculating the ADP, Employer Contributions on behalf of any Participant will include: 1) any Elective Deferrals (other than Catch-up Contributions or Elective Deferrals subsequently distributed as a permissible withdrawal) made pursuant to the Participant’s salary deferral election or pursuant to automatic Elective Deferral enrollment, if applicable (including Excess Elective Deferrals of Highly Compensated Employees), but excluding a) Excess Elective Deferrals of Participants who are non-Highly Compensated Employees that arise solely from Elective Deferrals made under the Plan or plans of this Employer and b) Elective Deferrals that are taken into account in the Actual Deferral Percentage test (provided that the ADP test is satisfied both before and after exclusion of these Elective Deferrals); and 2) if elected by the Employer, Qualified Nonelective Contributions and/or Qualified Matching Contributions. For purposes of computing Actual Deferral Percentages, an Employee who would be a Participant but for the failure to make Elective Deferrals will be treated as a Participant on whose behalf no Elective Deferrals are made. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the Actual Deferral Percentage (ADP) related to matching contributions, but they differ in structure and specific options available. The Relius provision focuses on the ADP definition and includes specific matching contribution options, while the Ascensus provision provides a more detailed calculation method for ADP and includes additional options related to eligibility and contributions.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Ascensus provision's detailed calculation of ADP and inclusion of Qualified Nonelective Contributions, which are not mentioned in the Relius provision. Additionally, the Relius provision has specific matching contribution options that are not directly mirrored in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: "Administrator" means the Employer unless another person, entity or committee has been designated by the Employer pursuant to Section 2.2 to administer the Plan on behalf of the Employer. "Administrator" also includes any Qualified Termination Administrator (QTA) that has assumed the responsibilities of the Administrator in accordance with guidelines set forth by the Department of Labor. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Adopting Employer will be the Plan Administrator unless the managing body of the Adopting Employer designates a person or persons other than the Adopting Employer as the Plan Administrator and so notifies the Trustee (or Custodian, if applicable). The managing body of the Adopting Employer may also appoint a successor Plan Administrator. The Adopting Employer will also be the Plan Administrator if the person or persons so designated ceases to be the Plan Administrator and a successor Plan Administrator is not appointed. The Adopting Employer may establish an administrative committee that will carry out the Plan Administrator’s duties. Members of the administrative committee may allocate the Plan Administrator’s duties among themselves. If the managing body of the Adopting Employer designates a person or persons other than the Adopting Employer as Plan Administrator, such person or persons will serve at the pleasure of the Adopting Employer and will serve pursuant to such procedures as such managing body may provide. Each such person will be bound as may be required by law. The Plan Administrator will include any person authorized to perform the duties of the Plan Administrator and properly identified to the Trustee or Custodian as such. Where the Adopting Employer dies, becomes incapacitated, or is otherwise unable to fulfill its duties, and neither the Adopting Employer nor the managing body of the Adopting Employer will appoint a successor Plan Administrator, the Adopting Employer may appoint a successor Plan Administrator within a reasonable period of time thereafter. The Plan Administrator may appoint a successor Plan Administrator. Where the Plan Administrator will not or cannot appoint a successor Plan Administrator, a majority of Participants in the Plan will have the authority to appoint a successor Plan Administrator but will not be obligated to do so if engaging a majority of Participants would result in unreasonable time, expense, or administrative burden. The pre-approved Document Provider will in no case be designated as the Plan Administrator. The Plan Administrator must ensure that the authority over the portion of the Fund subject to the trust requirements of ERISA section 403(a) is assigned to a Trustee (subject to the proper and lawful directions of the Plan Administrator), or an investment manager. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define the role of the Plan Administrator, but they differ in structure and detail regarding the appointment and responsibilities of the Administrator. The Relius provision is more concise and includes specific references to a Qualified Termination Administrator, while the Ascensus provision provides a more detailed process for appointing and replacing the Plan Administrator, including the role of an administrative committee and participant involvement.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Relius provision's mention of a Qualified Termination Administrator and the Ascensus provision's detailed process for appointing a successor Plan Administrator and the potential for participant involvement in that process.

relius_provision_text: "Adoption Agreement" means the separate agreement which is executed by the Employer and sets forth the elective provisions of this Plan as specified by the Employer. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Employer elects to take Qualified Matching Contributions into account as Elective Deferrals for purposes of the ADP test, then (subject to such other requirements as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury) the Employer may elect, in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, to either include all Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test or to include only the amount of such Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions in retirement plans, but they govern different aspects and have different structures. The Relius provision focuses on the adoption agreement and specific matching contribution types, while the Ascensus provision discusses Qualified Matching Contributions and their treatment for ADP testing. The options available in each are not directly comparable, as they pertain to different requirements and contexts.
variance_notes: Relius includes specific matching contribution types and conditions, while Ascensus focuses on the treatment of Qualified Matching Contributions for ADP testing. Additionally, Relius mentions safe harbor contributions, which are not addressed in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: "Affiliated Employer" means any corporation which is a member of a controlled group of corporations (as defined in Code §414(b)) which includes the Employer; any trade or business (whether or not incorporated) which is under common control (as defined in Code §414(c)) with the Employer; any organization (whether or not incorporated) which is a member of an affiliated service group (as defined in Code §414(m)) which includes the Employer; and any other entity required to be aggregated with the Employer pursuant to Regulations under Code §414(o). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means an employer who, along with another employer, is a member of 1) a controlled group of corporations (as defined in Code section 414(b) as modified by Code section 414(h)), 2) a commonly controlled trade or business (as defined in Code section 414(c) as modified by Code section 414(h)(4)) or 3) an affiliated service group (as defined in Code section 414(m)) (and any other entity required to be aggregated with another employer pursuant to Treasury regulations under Code section 414(o)). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define a related employer in the context of controlled groups, commonly controlled trades, and affiliated service groups under the same sections of the Internal Revenue Code. They govern the same plan feature regarding employer contributions.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision includes a modification reference to Code section 414(h), which is not present in the Relius provision. Additionally, the related adoption agreement elections differ significantly, with Relius focusing on employer nonelective contributions while Ascensus lists eligibility and allocation formulas.

relius_provision_text: "Affirmative Election" means a Salary Deferral Agreement submitted by a Participant to the Administrator in accordance with Section 12.2 that provides instructions to defer a specified amount of Compensation (including an affirmative election to defer no amount) as an Elective Deferral to the Plan. A Participant's Affirmative Election is generally effective as of the first payroll period which follows the payroll period in which the Participant made the Affirmative Election. However, a Participant may make an Affirmative Election which is effective: (a) for the first payroll period in which he or she becomes a Participant and the Participant makes an Affirmative Election within a reasonable period following the Participant's becoming eligible to make Elective Deferrals and before the Compensation to which the Election applies becomes currently available; or (b) for the first payroll period following the effective date of the Automatic Contribution Arrangement if the Participant makes an Affirmative Election not later than the Automatic Contribution Arrangement's effective date. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Employer elects to take Qualified Matching Contributions into account as Elective Deferrals for purposes of the ADP test, then (subject to such other requirements as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury) the Employer may elect, in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, to either include all Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test or to include only the amount of such Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions in retirement plans, but they govern different aspects. The Relius provision focuses on Affirmative Elections for Elective Deferrals, while the Ascensus provision discusses Qualified Matching Contributions in relation to the ADP test. The options available in the adoption agreements also differ significantly, with Relius providing specific matching contribution structures and Ascensus focusing on eligibility and contribution types.
variance_notes: Key differences include the focus on Affirmative Elections in Relius versus Qualified Matching Contributions in Ascensus, and the specific options available for matching contributions differ between the two templates.

relius_provision_text: "Alternate Payee" means an alternate payee pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order that meets the requirements of Code §414(p). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means any Spouse, former Spouse, child, or other dependent of a Participant who is recognized by a Domestic Relations Order as having a right to receive all, or a portion of, the benefits payable under the Plan with respect to such Participant. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to alternate payees under domestic relations orders, but they define 'Alternate Payee' differently. The Relius provision specifically references qualified domestic relations orders under Code §414(p), while the Ascensus provision includes a broader definition that encompasses spouses, former spouses, children, or other dependents without specific reference to the Code. Additionally, the related adoption agreement elections differ significantly in structure and purpose, indicating that they do not govern the same plan feature/requirement.
variance_notes: Key differences include the specific legal reference in Relius and the broader definition in Ascensus. The related elections also differ in focus, with Relius emphasizing compensation types and Ascensus focusing on contribution options.

relius_title: POWERS AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF THE EMPLOYER
relius_provision_text: (b) Funding policy and method. The Employer shall establish a "funding policy and method", i.e., it shall determine whether the Plan has a short run need for liquidity (e.g., to pay benefits) or whether liquidity is a long run goal and investment growth (and stability of same) is a more current need, or shall appoint a qualified person to do so. If the Trustee (or Insurer) has discretionary authority, the Employer or its delegate shall communicate such needs and goals to the Trustee (or Insurer), who shall coordinate such Plan needs with its investment policy. The communication of such a 'funding policy and method' shall not, however, constitute a directive to the Trustee (or Insurer) as to the investment of the Trust Funds. Such 'funding policy and method' shall be consistent with the objectives of this Plan and with the requirements of Title I of the Act. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: A. Plan Investments – Except as provided in Plan Section 7.22(B) (relating to individual direction of investments by Participants), the Adopting Employer, not the Trustee (or Custodian, if applicable), will have exclusive management and control over the investment of the Fund into any permitted investment. The Adopting Employer will be responsible for establishing a funding policy statement on behalf of the Plan and shall provide a copy of such funding policy statement to the Trustee who has the authority or discretion to select the appropriate investments for the Fund, if any. Notwithstanding the preceding, if the Trustee has the authority or discretion to select the appropriate investments for the Fund, such Trustee may enter into an agreement with the Adopting Employer whereby the Trustee will manage the investment of all or a portion of the Fund. Any such agreement will be in writing and will set forth such matters as such Trustee deems necessary or desirable. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the management and control of plan investments, emphasizing the employer's role in establishing a funding policy. They both allow for communication with the trustee regarding investment needs, although the Relius provision is more focused on liquidity needs while the Ascensus provision emphasizes exclusive management by the employer.
variance_notes: The Relius provision allows for the possibility of appointing a qualified person for funding policy, while the Ascensus provision explicitly states that the employer has exclusive management and control. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes a clause about the trustee potentially managing investments under an agreement, which is not mentioned in the Relius provision.

relius_title: POWERS AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF THE EMPLOYER
relius_provision_text: (c) Appointment of Investment Manager. Unless prohibited by the terms of the Trust agreement, the Employer may appoint, at its option, one or more Investment Managers, investment advisers, or other agents to provide investment direction to the Trustee (or Insurer) with respect to any or all of the Plan assets. Such appointment shall be given by the Employer in writing in a form acceptable to the Trustee (or Insurer) and shall specifically identify the Plan assets with respect to which the Investment Manager or other agent shall have the authority to direct the investment. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: A. Plan Investments – Except as provided in Plan Section 7.22(B) (relating to individual direction of investments by Participants), the Adopting Employer, not the Trustee (or Custodian, if applicable), will have exclusive management and control over the investment of the Fund into any permitted investment. The Adopting Employer will be responsible for establishing a funding policy statement on behalf of the Plan and shall provide a copy of such funding policy statement to the Trustee who has the authority or discretion to select the appropriate investments for the Fund, if any. Notwithstanding the preceding, if the Trustee has the authority or discretion to select the appropriate investments for the Fund, such Trustee may enter into an agreement with the Adopting Employer whereby the Trustee will manage the investment of all or a portion of the Fund. Any such agreement will be in writing and will set forth such matters as such Trustee deems necessary or desirable. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the management and control of plan investments, but they differ in the roles assigned to the Employer and Trustee. The Relius provision allows the Employer to appoint Investment Managers, while the Ascensus provision emphasizes the Employer's exclusive management and control, with the Trustee having a more passive role unless an agreement is made. This indicates a difference in the structure of investment management.
variance_notes: The Relius provision explicitly allows for the appointment of Investment Managers, while the Ascensus provision focuses on the Employer's control and the Trustee's potential role in managing investments only through an agreement. This could lead to different outcomes in practice regarding investment management.

relius_title: POWERS AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF THE EMPLOYER
relius_provision_text: (a) Appointment of Trustee (or Insurer) and Administrator. In addition to the general powers and responsibilities otherwise provided for in this Plan, the Employer shall be empowered to appoint and remove one or more Trustees or (Insurers) and Administrators from time to time as it deems necessary for the proper administration of the Plan to ensure that the Plan is being operated for the exclusive benefit of the Participants and their Beneficiaries in accordance with the terms of the Plan, the Code, and the Act. The Employer may appoint counsel, specialists, advisers, agents (including any nonfiduciary and) and other persons as the Employer deems necessary or desirable in connection with the exercise of its fiduciary duties under this Plan. The Employer may compensate such agents or advisers from the assets of the Plan as fiduciary expenses (but not including any business (settlor) expenses of the Employer), to the extent not paid by the Employer. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Adopting Employer will be the Plan Administrator unless the managing body of the Adopting Employer designates a person or persons other than the Adopting Employer as the Plan Administrator and so notifies the Trustee (or Custodian, if applicable). The managing body of the Adopting Employer may also appoint a successor Plan Administrator. The Adopting Employer will also be the Plan Administrator if the person or persons so designated ceases to be the Plan Administrator and a successor Plan Administrator is not appointed. The Adopting Employer may establish an administrative committee that will carry out the Plan Administrator’s duties. Members of the administrative committee may allocate the Plan Administrator’s duties among themselves. If the managing body of the Adopting Employer designates a person or persons other than the Adopting Employer as Plan Administrator, such person or persons will serve at the pleasure of the Adopting Employer and will serve pursuant to such procedures as such managing body may provide. Each such person will be bound as may be required by law. The Plan Administrator will include any person authorized to perform the duties of the Plan Administrator and properly identified to the Trustee or Custodian as such. Where the Adopting Employer dies, becomes incapacitated, or is otherwise unable to fulfill its duties, and neither the Adopting Employer nor the managing body of the Adopting Employer will appoint a successor Plan Administrator, the Adopting Employer may appoint a successor Plan Administrator within a reasonable period of time thereafter. The Plan Administrator may appoint a successor Plan Administrator. Where the Plan Administrator will not or cannot appoint a successor Plan Administrator, a majority of Participants in the Plan will have the authority to appoint a successor Plan Administrator but will not be obligated to do so if engaging a majority of Participants would result in unreasonable time, expense, or administrative burden. The pre-approved Document Provider will in no case be designated as the Plan Administrator. The Plan Administrator must ensure that the authority over the portion of the Fund subject to the trust requirements of ERISA section 403(a) is assigned to a Trustee (subject to the proper and lawful directions of the Plan Administrator), or an investment manager. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to the administration of the retirement plan, but they focus on different aspects. The Relius provision emphasizes the powers and responsibilities of the Employer in appointing trustees and administrators, while the Ascensus provision details the role of the Adopting Employer as the Plan Administrator and the process for appointing successors. Although both involve administrative roles, they do not govern the same specific plan feature.
variance_notes: The Relius provision discusses the appointment and removal of trustees and administrators, while the Ascensus provision focuses on the designation of the Plan Administrator and the establishment of an administrative committee. The Relius provision allows for compensation of agents from plan assets, which is not addressed in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: The Employer may appoint one or more Administrators. If the Employer does not appoint an Administrator, the Employer will be the Administrator. Any person, including, but not limited to, the Employees of the Employer, shall be eligible to serve as an Administrator. Any person so appointed shall signify acceptance by filing written or electronic acceptance with the Employer. An Administrator may resign by delivering a written resignation to the Employer or be removed by the Employer by delivery of written notice of removal, to take effect at a date specified therein, or upon delivery to the Administrator if no date is specified. Upon the resignation or removal of an Administrator, the Employer may designate a successor to this position. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]

relius_provision_text: If more than one person is appointed as Administrator, then the responsibilities of each Administrator may be specified by the Employer and accepted in writing by each Administrator. ascensus_provision_text: The Adopting Employer will be the Plan Administrator unless the managing body of the Adopting Employer designates a person or persons other than the Adopting Employer as the Plan Administrator and so notifies the Trustee (or Custodian, if applicable). The managing body of the Adopting Employer may also appoint a successor Plan Administrator. The Adopting Employer will also be the Plan Administrator if the person or persons so designated ceases to be the Plan Administrator and a successor Plan Administrator is not appointed. The Adopting Employer may establish an administrative committee that will carry out the Plan Administrator’s duties. Members of the administrative committee may allocate the Plan Administrator’s duties among themselves. If the managing body of the Adopting Employer designates a person or persons other than the Adopting Employer as Plan Administrator, such person or persons will serve at the pleasure of the Adopting Employer and will serve pursuant to such procedures as such managing body may provide. Each such person will be bound as may be required by law. The Plan Administrator will include any person authorized to perform the duties of the Plan Administrator and properly identified to the Trustee or Custodian as such. Where the Adopting Employer dies, becomes incapacitated, or is otherwise unable to fulfill its duties, and neither the Adopting Employer nor the managing body of the Adopting Employer will appoint a successor Plan Administrator, the Adopting Employer may appoint a successor Plan Administrator within a reasonable period of time thereafter. The Plan Administrator may appoint a successor Plan Administrator. Where the Plan Administrator will not or cannot appoint a successor Plan Administrator, a majority of Participants in the Plan will have the authority to appoint a successor Plan Administrator but will not be obligated to do so if engaging a majority of Participants would result in unreasonable time, expense, or administrative burden. The pre-approved Document Provider will in no case be designated as the Plan Administrator. The Plan Administrator must ensure that the authority over the portion of the Fund subject to the trust requirements of ERISA section 403(a) is assigned to a Trustee (subject to the proper and lawful directions of the Plan Administrator), or an investment manager. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]

relius_provision_text:
All reasonable expenses of administration may be paid out of the Plan assets unless paid by the Employer. Such expenses shall include any expenses incident to the functioning of the Administrator, or any person or persons retained or appointed by any named Fiduciary incident to the exercise of their duties under the Plan, including, but not limited to, fees of accountants, counsel, Investment Managers, agents (including nonfiduciary agents) appointed for the purpose of assisting the Administrator or Trustee (or Insurer) in carrying out the instructions of Participants as to the directed investment of their Accounts (if permitted) and other specialists and their agents, the costs of any bonds required pursuant to Act §412, and other costs of administering the Plan.

If liquid assets of the Plan are insufficient to cover the fees of the Trustee (or Insurer) or the Administrator, the Plan assets shall be liquidated to the extent necessary for such fees. In the event any part of the Plan assets becomes subject to tax, all taxes incurred will be paid from the Plan assets. Until paid, the expenses shall constitute a liability of the Trust Fund.

Expenses may be charged to Account. Unless specifically prohibited under statute, regulation or other guidance of general applicability, the Administrator may charge to the Account of an individual Participant a reasonable charge to offset the cost of making a distribution to the Participant, Beneficiary, or Alternate Payee. Means the maximum Annual Addition that may be contributed or allocated to a Participant’s Individual Account under the Plan for any Limitation Year.
