Additionally, Relius mentions specific procedures for short-term employment termination, which is not addressed in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: 410(b) ratio percentage fail-safe provisions. Notwithstanding anything in this Section to the contrary, the provisions of this Subsection apply for any Plan Year if, in the non-standardized Adoption Agreement, the Employer elected to apply the 410(b) ratio percentage fail-safe provisions and the Plan fails to satisfy the 'ratio percentage test' due to a last day of the Plan Year allocation condition or an Hours of Service (or months of service) allocation condition. A plan will fail the ratio test if the plan's 'benefiting ratio' of the Nonhighly Compensated Employees who are 'includible' is at least 70% of the 'benefiting ratio' of the Highly Compensated Employees who are 'includible'. The 'benefiting ratio' of the Nonhighly Compensated Employees is the number of 'includible' Nonhighly Compensated Employees who are 'benefiting' under the Plan divided by the number of 'includible' Employees who are Nonhighly Compensated Employees. The 'benefiting ratio' of the Highly Compensated Employees is the number of 'includible' Highly Compensated Employees who are 'benefiting' under the Plan divided by the number of 'includible' Highly Compensated Employees. The 'includible' Employees are all Employees other than: (1) those Employees excluded from participating in the Plan for the entire Plan Year by reason of the collective bargaining unit exclusion or the nonresident alien exclusion described in the Code or by reason of the age and service requirements of Article III; and (2) any Employee who incurs a separation from service during the Plan Year and fails to complete at least 501 Hours of Service (or three (3) months of service if the elapsed time method is being used) during such Plan Year. For purposes of this Subsection, an Employee is 'benefiting' under the Plan on a particular date if, under the Plan, the Employee is entitled to an Employer contribution or an allocation of Forfeitures for the Plan Year. If this Subsection applies and the Hours of Service method is used, then the Administrator will suspend the allocation conditions for all such 'includible' Employees and will allow them to continue participating in the Plan year to share in the contribution, beginning with the 'includible' Employees employed by the Employer on the last day of the Plan Year who have completed the greatest number of Hours of Service in the Plan Year, and continuing to suspend the allocation conditions for each 'includible' Employee who completed Hours of Service, from the greatest number of Hours of Service to the least, until the Plan satisfies the 'ratio percentage test' for the Plan Year. If two or more 'includible' Employees have the same number of Hours of Service, then the Administrator will suspend the allocation conditions for all such 'includible' Employees, irrespective of whether the Plan can satisfy the 'ratio percentage test' by accruing benefits for fewer than all such 'includible' Employees. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Minimum Coverage Test – Notwithstanding anything in the Plan to the contrary, the Adopting Employer may use either the ratio percentage test (and the correction option described below, if applicable) or the average benefits test to satisfy the minimum coverage requirements. This paragraph may apply for any Plan Year that the Plan fails to satisfy the ratio percentage test described in Code section 410(b)(1) as of the last day of any such Plan Year. The ratio percentage test is satisfied if, on the last day of the Plan Year, taking into account all Employees, or former Employees who were employed by the Employer on any day during the Plan Year, either the Plan benefits at least 70 percent of the percentage of Employees who are not Highly Compensated Employees or the Plan benefits a percentage of Employees who are not Highly Compensated Employees that is at least 70 percent of the percentage of Highly Compensated Employees benefiting under the Plan. A Participant is treated as benefiting under the Plan for any Plan Year during which the Participant received or is deemed to receive an allocation in accordance with Code section 1.410(b)-3(a). If the Plan fails the ratio percentage test, the Employer Contribution for the Plan Year may be allocated to an individual Participant, and any similarly situated Participants to the extent necessary to satisfy that minimum coverage test. For example, if the Employer Contribution is allocated to all individuals in the first class of Participants and the Plan still fails, the Employer Contribution will be allocated to an individual Participant and any other similarly situated Participants in the second class of Participants set forth below. The classes of Participants to be included are as follows: i. Each Participant who is still employed on the last day of the Plan Year starting with the Participant who has completed either the highest number of Hours of Service during the Plan Year, if the Hours of Service method of determining service is used; or the highest number of days worked during the Plan Year, if the Elapsed Time method of determining service is used; ii. Each Participant who is not employed on the last day of the Plan Year because the Participant has died, incurred a Disability, or attained Normal Retirement Age; iii. Each Participant who is not employed on the last day of the Plan Year starting with the Participant who has completed either the highest number of Hours of Service during the Plan Year, if the Hours of Service method of determining service is used; or the highest number of days worked during the Plan Year, if the Elapsed Time method of determining service is used. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the minimum coverage requirements under the ratio percentage test as outlined in Code section 410(b). They provide mechanisms for correcting failures in the ratio percentage test by allowing contributions to be allocated to certain participants based on their service. The structure and intent of both provisions are similar, focusing on ensuring that a sufficient percentage of non-highly compensated employees benefit from the plan.
variance_notes: The Relius provision specifies the conditions under which the allocation conditions are suspended and the order of allocation based on hours of service, while the Ascensus provision outlines a more detailed approach to allocating contributions to 'similarly situated Participants' and includes additional classes of participants. The Ascensus provision also explicitly mentions the option to use the average benefits test, which is not mentioned in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Employer may increase the "gateway contribution" to satisfy the provisions of Regulation §1.401(a)(4)-(9)(b)(2)(v)(D) if the plan (for nondiscrimination testing purposes) consists of one or more defined contribution plans and one or more defined benefit plans. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Plan is a money purchase pension plan, Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions will be designated as Employer Money Purchase Pension Plan Contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions, but they govern different types of contributions and have different focuses. The Relius provision discusses increasing a 'gateway contribution' for nondiscrimination testing in the context of defined contribution and defined benefit plans, while the Ascensus provision specifically addresses the designation of Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions within a money purchase pension plan.
variance_notes: The Relius provision is broader, allowing for adjustments to contributions for compliance, whereas the Ascensus provision is more specific to categorizing contributions within a particular plan type.

relius_title: Effective date of the withdrawal election.
relius_provision_text: The effective date of the permissible withdrawal will be as soon as practicable, but in no event later than the earlier of (A) the pay date of the second payroll period beginning after the election is made, or (B) the first pay date that occurs at least thirty (30) days after the election is made. The election will also be deemed to be an Affirmative Election to have no Elective Deferrals made to the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Permissible Withdrawals – Elective Deferrals made according to the Plan under either the EACA or the QACA provisions may be withdrawn as permissible withdrawals in accordance with Plan Section 5.01(A)(2). The conditions require an election by the Participant, and the timing of the withdrawal must align with the Adoption Agreement (generally within 30 days after the first Elective Deferral under the EACA or QACA). The effective date of the withdrawal will be no later than the earlier of (i) the pay date for the second payroll period after the election, or (ii) the first pay date that occurs at least 30 days after the election. The provisions may also address matching contributions and related plan provisions as they relate to permissible withdrawals. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the effective date of permissible withdrawals related to Elective Deferrals, with similar timing requirements for the effective date. They also reference matching contributions in relation to permissible withdrawals.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision explicitly mentions EACA and QACA provisions, while the Relius provision does not specify these terms. Additionally, the related adoption agreement elections differ in focus, with Relius emphasizing matching contributions and Ascensus focusing on eligibility requirements.

relius_provision_text: The Administrator will not take any Elective Deferrals withdrawn pursuant to this Section into account in computing and allocating matching contributions. If the Employer has already allocated matching contributions to the Participant's Account with respect to Elective Deferrals being withdrawn pursuant to this Subsection (4), then such matching contributions, as adjusted for gains and losses, must be forfeited. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Employer elects to take Qualified Matching Contributions into account as Elective Deferrals for purposes of the ADP test, then (subject to such other requirements as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury) the Employer may elect, in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, to either include all Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test or to include only the amount of such Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions, but they govern different aspects of how those contributions are treated in relation to Elective Deferrals and ADP testing. The Relius provision focuses on the treatment of Elective Deferrals withdrawn and the forfeiture of matching contributions, while the Ascensus provision discusses the inclusion of Qualified Matching Contributions for ADP testing purposes.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Relius provision's emphasis on forfeiture of matching contributions upon withdrawal of Elective Deferrals, whereas the Ascensus provision allows for the inclusion of Qualified Matching Contributions in ADP testing, which is not addressed in the Relius text.

relius_provision_text: With regard to Elective Deferrals withdrawn pursuant to this Subsection, (A) the Administrator will disregard such Elective Deferrals in the Actual Deferral Percentage Test (if applicable); (B) the Administrator will disregard such Elective Deferrals for purposes of the limitation on Elective Deferrals under Code §402(g); (C) such Elective Deferrals are not subject to the consent requirements of Code §401(a)(11) or 417. The Administrator will disregard any matching contributions forfeited under paragraph (vi) above in the ACP Test (if applicable). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Employer elects to take Elective Deferrals into account as Contribution Percentage Amounts for purposes of the ACP test, then (subject to such other requirements as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury) the Employer may elect, in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, either to include all Elective Deferrals in the ACP test or to include only the amount of such Elective Deferrals that are needed to meet the ACP test. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the treatment of Elective Deferrals in relation to matching contributions and the ACP test, but they differ in their specific requirements and options for handling Elective Deferrals. The Relius provision explicitly states how Elective Deferrals are disregarded in various tests, while the Ascensus provision allows for more flexibility in how Elective Deferrals can be included in the ACP test.
variance_notes: Relius provides specific disregard rules for Elective Deferrals in the ACP test and other limitations, while Ascensus allows for a choice in how Elective Deferrals are treated for the ACP test, indicating a difference in approach to compliance and testing.

relius_provision_text: A Participant's Affirmative Election continues in effect until the Participant subsequently revokes or modifies his or her Salary Deferral Agreement, or the Affirmative Election no longer applies (e.g., due to re-enrollment). A Participant who has an Affirmative Election in effect is not thereafter subject to the Automatic Deferral or to any scheduled increases thereto, even if the Participant later revokes the Affirmative Election, unless the Participant is subject to the EACA. In addition, a Participant who is subject to the EACA provisions who revokes his or her Affirmative Election, will be deemed to have made an Affirmative Election to have no Elective Deferrals made to the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Adopting Employer so elects in the Adoption Agreement, the Elective Deferral percentage or amount for Contributing Participants who are automatically enrolled pursuant to the ACA and EACA Plan provisions will be adjusted automatically by the Employer in the increments and time periods stated in the Adoption Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions and automatic deferral adjustments, but they govern different aspects of participant elections and the implications of those elections. The Relius provision focuses on the effect of an Affirmative Election and its revocation, while the Ascensus provision addresses automatic adjustments for participants who are automatically enrolled.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific details about the revocation of an Affirmative Election and its implications under EACA, which is not addressed in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Ascensus provision emphasizes automatic adjustments for participants, which is not a focus in the Relius text.

relius_provision_text: With respect to a non-standardized Adoption Agreement, an individual shall not be an Eligible Employee (unless otherwise elected in Appendix A to the Adoption Agreement) if such individual is a "Reclassified Employee." A "Reclassified Employee" is any person the Employer does not treat as a common law employee or as a self-employed individual (including, but not limited to, independent contractors, persons the Employer pays outside of this payroll system and out-sourced workers) for federal income tax withholding purposes under Code §401(a), irrespective of whether there is a binding determination that the individual is an Employee or a Leased Employee of the Employer. Self-Employed Individuals are not "Reclassified Employees." [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will determine the eligibility of each Employee to be a Participant. This determination will be conclusive and binding upon all persons except as otherwise provided herein or by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address eligibility for participation in a retirement plan, but they do so in fundamentally different ways. The Relius provision specifically defines 'Reclassified Employees' and excludes them from eligibility, while the Ascensus provision grants the Plan Administrator broad discretion to determine eligibility without specific exclusions. This indicates a difference in approach to eligibility criteria.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific definitions and exclusions (e.g., Reclassified Employees), while the Ascensus provision emphasizes the authority of the Plan Administrator to make eligibility determinations, which may or may not align with the exclusions in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: Affiliated Employers. With respect to a non-standardized Adoption Agreement, Employees of an Affiliated Employer will not be treated as Eligible Employees prior to the date the Affiliated Employer adopts the Plan as a Participating Employer. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will determine the eligibility of each Employee to be a Participant. This determination will be conclusive and binding upon all persons except as otherwise provided herein or by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address eligibility for participation in a retirement plan, but they do so in different ways. The Relius provision specifies that employees of an Affiliated Employer are not eligible until the employer adopts the plan, while the Ascensus provision gives the Plan Administrator the authority to determine eligibility, which is more general and does not specifically mention Affiliated Employers.
variance_notes: The Relius provision has a specific condition related to Affiliated Employers, while the Ascensus provision focuses on the Plan Administrator's discretion in determining eligibility. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes options related to specific elections and contributions that are not present in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: Any organization may become the Employer hereunder by executing the Adoption Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means any corporation, sole proprietor, or other entity named in the Adoption Agreement and any successor who by merger, consolidation, purchase, or otherwise assumes the obligations of the Plan. The Adopting Employer will be a named fiduciary for purposes of ERISA section 402(a). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions and the adoption of the plan, but they focus on different aspects. The Relius provision specifically addresses the method of adoption and employer nonelective contributions, while the Ascensus provision defines the adopting employer and their fiduciary role without detailing contribution types.
variance_notes: The Relius provision is more focused on the types of contributions (nonelective, profit sharing, etc.), whereas the Ascensus provision emphasizes the definition of the adopting employer and their responsibilities under ERISA. The options available in the related adoption agreement elections also differ significantly.

relius_provision_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Plan, the affiliation of the Employer and the participation of its Participants shall be separate and apart from that of any other employer and its participants hereunder. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will determine the eligibility of each Employee to be a Participant. This determination will be conclusive and binding upon all persons except as otherwise provided herein or by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address eligibility for participation in a retirement plan, but they do so in different ways. The Relius provision emphasizes the separation of employer affiliations and specific eligibility conditions, while the Ascensus provision grants the Plan Administrator the authority to determine eligibility, which is more centralized and less focused on employer affiliation.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific conditions for eligibility and mentions separate affiliations, while the Ascensus provision allows for a broader determination of eligibility by the Plan Administrator without reference to employer affiliations.

relius_provision_text: (a) General rule. Subject to the exceptions provided below and as otherwise permitted by the Code and the Act, no benefit which shall be payable to any person (including a Participant or the Participant's Beneficiary) shall be subject to any anticipation, alienation, sale, transfer, assignment, pledge, encumbrance, or charge, and any attempt to anticipate, alienate, sell, transfer, assign, pledge, encumber, or charge the same shall be void; and no such benefit shall be liable for, or subject to the debts, contracts, liabilities, engagements, or torts of any such person, nor shall it be subject to attachment or legal process for or against such person, and the same shall not be recognized except to such extent as may be required by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
No benefit or interest available under the Plan will be subject to assignment or alienation, either voluntarily or involuntarily. The preceding sentence will not apply to judgments and settlements described in Code section 401(a)(13)(C) and ERISA section 206(d)(4). Such sentence will, however, apply to the creation, assignment, or recognition of a right to any benefit payable with respect to a Participant pursuant to a Domestic Relations Order, unless such order is determined to be a Qualified Domestic Relations Order as defined in the Definitions section of the Plan.

Generally, a Domestic Relations Order cannot be a Qualified Domestic Relations Order until January 1, 1985. However, in the case of a Domestic Relations Order entered before January 1, 1985, the Plan Administrator:

1. shall treat such order as a Qualified Domestic Relations Order if the Plan Administrator is paying benefits pursuant to such order on January 1, 1985; and

2. may treat any other such order entered before January 1, 1985, as a Qualified Domestic Relations Order even if such order does not meet the requirements of Code section 414(p).

Notwithstanding any provision of the Plan to the contrary, a distribution to an Alternate Payee under a Qualified Domestic Relations Order will be permitted even if the Participant affected by such order is not otherwise entitled to a distribution, and even if such Participant has not attained the earliest retirement age as defined in Code section 414(p). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the inalienability of benefits under retirement plans, but they differ in specific legal references and the treatment of Domestic Relations Orders. The Relius provision is broader in its prohibition against alienation, while the Ascensus provision includes specific exceptions for Domestic Relations Orders and provides additional details on how such orders are treated.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Relius provision's general prohibition against alienation without exceptions (except as required by law), while the Ascensus provision explicitly allows for exceptions related to Domestic Relations Orders and provides conditions under which such orders may be treated as Qualified Domestic Relations Orders.

relius_title: Exception for certain debts to Plan.
relius_provision_text: (d) Exception for certain debts to Plan. Notwithstanding any provision of this Section to the contrary, an offset to a Participant's accrued benefit against an amount that the Participant is ordered or required to pay the Plan with respect to a judgment, order, or decree issued, or a settlement entered into, on or after August 5, 1997, shall be permitted in accordance with Code §§401(a)(13)(C) and (D). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
A Domestic Relations Order will not be treated as failing to meet the requirements above solely because such order requires that payment of benefits be made to an Alternate Payee

1. on or after the date on which the Participant attains (or would have attained) the earliest retirement age as defined in Code section 414(p)(4)(B),

2. as if the Participant had retired on the date on which such payment is to begin under such order, and

3. in any form in which such benefits may be paid under the Plan to the Participant (other than in a Qualified Joint and Survivor Annuity) with respect to the Alternate Payee and their subsequent spouse. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address exceptions related to payments from a retirement plan, but they focus on different aspects. The Relius provision specifically discusses offsets for debts to the Plan due to legal orders, while the Ascensus provision pertains to the treatment of Domestic Relations Orders and payments to Alternate Payees. They do not govern the same plan feature/requirement.
variance_notes: The Relius provision is about offsets for participant debts, while the Ascensus provision is about payments to Alternate Payees under Domestic Relations Orders. The options/elections available in the adoption agreements also differ significantly in focus.

relius_provision_text: (a) Applicable law. This Plan shall be constructed and enforced according to the Code, the Act and the laws of the state or commonwealth in which the Employer's principal office is located (unless otherwise designated in Appendix A to the Adoption Agreement (Special Effective Dates and Other Permitted Elections)), other than its laws respecting choice of law, to the extent not pre-empted by federal law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
To the extent such laws are not preempted by federal law, the terms and conditions of this Plan will be governed by the laws of the state in which the Pre-approved Document Provider is located, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Pre-approved Document Provider and the Employer.

In the event of any conflict between the provisions of this Basic Plan Document and provisions of the Adoption Agreement, the summary plan description, or any related documents, the Basic Plan Document will control. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the governing laws of the plan, but they differ in their specific language and context. The Relius provision focuses on the laws of the state where the Employer's principal office is located, while the Ascensus provision refers to the laws of the state where the Pre-approved Document Provider is located. Additionally, the Relius provision includes specific references to the Code and the Act, which are not mentioned in the Ascensus provision.
variance_notes: The Relius provision explicitly mentions the Code and the Act, while the Ascensus provision does not. The governing law references differ based on the location of the Employer versus the Document Provider, which could lead to different legal interpretations or applications.

relius_provision_text: (b) Administrator's discretion/nondiscriminatory administration. The Administrator has total and complete discretion to interpret and construe the Plan and to determine all questions arising in the administration, interpretation and application of the Plan. Any determination the Administrator makes under the Plan is final and binding upon any affected person. The Administrator must exercise all of its Plan powers and discretion, and perform all of its duties, in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner.
ascensus_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF THE PLAN ADMINISTRATOR
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will have the authority to control and manage the operation and administration of the Plan. The Plan Administrator shall administer the Plan for the exclusive benefit of the Participants and their Beneficiaries in accordance with the specific terms of the Plan.
reasoning: Both provisions address the authority and discretion of the Plan Administrator, but they emphasize different aspects. The Relius provision highlights the Administrator's discretion and the requirement for nondiscriminatory administration, while the Ascensus provision focuses on the exclusive benefit of Participants and their Beneficiaries. This indicates a difference in emphasis and scope.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes a specific requirement for nondiscriminatory administration and finality of decisions, which is not explicitly mentioned in the Ascensus provision. The Ascensus provision emphasizes the exclusive benefit of Participants, which is not a focus in the Relius text.

relius_provision_text:
(a) Permitted contributions. For each Plan Year, the Employer will (or may with respect to any discretionary contributions) contribute to the Plan:

(1) The amount of the total salary deferral elections of all Participants made pursuant to Section 12.2(a), which amount shall be deemed Elective Deferrals, plus

(2) If elected in the Adoption Agreement, a matching contribution equal to the percentage, if any, specified in the Adoption Agreement, of the Elective Deferrals of each Participant eligible to share in the allocations of the matching contribution, which amount shall be deemed an Employer matching contribution or Qualified Matching Contribution as elected in the Adoption Agreement, plus

(3) If elected in the Adoption Agreement, a discretionary amount determined each year by the Employer, which amount if any, shall be deemed an Employer Nonelective Contribution, or a "prevailing wage contribution" as set forth in the Adoption Agreement, which amount shall be an Employer Nonelective Contribution or a Qualified Nonelective Contribution as elected in the Adoption Agreement, plus

(4) A Qualified Nonelective Contribution in a discretionary amount, subject to the provisions of Section 12.12.

(5) Regardless of any provision in the Plan to the contrary, Employees whose employment is governed by a collective bargaining agreement between the Employer and "employee representatives" under which retirement benefits were the subject of good faith bargaining shall be eligible to participate in this Plan to the extent of employment covered by such agreement provided the agreement provides for coverage in the Plan. The contributions under this Plan shall be those set forth in the collective bargaining agreement and enumerated in the Adoption Agreement. For this purpose, the term "employee representatives" does not include any organization more than half of whose members are employees who are owners, officers, or executives of the Employer. The provisions of this Subsection only apply if no more than two percent (2%) of the Employees covered pursuant to the agreement are professionals as defined in Regulation §1.410(b)-9. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions made pursuant to the Safe Harbor CODA formula described in Adoption Agreement Section Three, in an amount equal to (i) 100 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that do not exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation for the Plan Year, plus (ii) 50 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation but that do not exceed five-percent of the Employee's Compensation, if applicable. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern matching contributions, but they differ in structure and specific requirements. The Relius provision allows for a broader range of contributions, including discretionary contributions and provisions for collective bargaining agreements, while the Ascensus provision is more narrowly defined with specific percentages for matching contributions.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Relius provision's allowance for discretionary contributions and collective bargaining agreements, which are not present in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the matching formula in Ascensus is more specific, detailing a tiered matching structure based on employee compensation percentages.

relius_provision_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, if the Employer is not a tax-exempt entity, then the Employer's contributions for any Fiscal Year may generally not exceed the maximum allowable as a deduction to the Employer under the provisions of Code §404. However, to the extent necessary to provide the top-heavy minimum allocations, the Employer shall make a contribution even if it exceeds current or accumulated net profit or the amount that is deductible under Code §404. Subject to the consent of the Trustee (or Insurer), the Employer may make its contribution to the Plan in the form of property, provided such contribution does not constitute a prohibited transaction under the Code or the Act. The decision to make a contribution of property is subject to the general fiduciary rules under the Act. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Except as otherwise elected in the Adoption Agreement, the Employer may contribute an amount to be determined from year to year. Unless otherwise elected in the Adoption Agreement, if this Plan is a profit sharing plan, the Employer may, in its sole discretion, make contributions without regard to current or accumulated earnings or profits. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern employer contributions to retirement plans, but they differ in their specific requirements and flexibility. The Relius provision explicitly limits contributions based on tax deductibility and top-heavy minimum allocations, while the Ascensus provision allows for contributions without regard to earnings or profits, indicating a broader discretion for the employer.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific references to tax code limitations and top-heavy rules, which are not present in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Ascensus provision allows for contributions to be determined year to year without the same constraints, suggesting a more flexible approach.

relius_provision_text: If elected in the Adoption Agreement, then effective as of the date specified in the Adoption Agreement, a Participant may elect an 'In-Plan Roth Rollover Contribution' (hereinafter referred to as an IRR) and/or an 'In-Plan Roth Transfer' (hereinafter referred to as an IRT) in accordance with the provisions of the Plan, this Section and the elections made in the Adoption Agreement. IRRs and IRTs will be subject to the Plan rules related to designated Roth accounts. Participants shall be fully Vested in the portion of their Accounts attributable to IRRs and IRTs. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: A. Eligibility – If elected by the Adopting Employer in the Adoption Agreement, Recipients may complete Direct In-Plan Roth Rollovers. Unless otherwise elected in the Adoption Agreement, a Plan that allows Direct In-Plan Roth Rollovers will allow Direct In-Plan Roth Rollovers of all non-Roth contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the same plan feature of allowing in-plan Roth rollovers, specifically addressing the eligibility and process for participants to make these contributions. They both require an election in the Adoption Agreement to enable this feature.
variance_notes: The Relius provision specifies both 'In-Plan Roth Rollover Contributions' and 'In-Plan Roth Transfers', while the Ascensus provision focuses on 'Direct In-Plan Roth Rollovers' of non-Roth contributions. The Relius provision also explicitly mentions vesting in the contributions, which is not addressed in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: Parts eligible to elect. The Employer in its Adoption Agreement can limit to Employees the right to elect to make IRRs or IRTs. If the Employer does not make this election, then for purposes of eligibility for an IRR, the Plan will treat a Participant's surviving spouse Beneficiary or alternate payee spouse or alternate payee Former spouse as a Participant. A non-spouse Beneficiary may not make an In-Plan Roth Rollover. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, for purposes of the Direct Rollover provisions of the Plan, an individual retirement account described in Code sections 408(a) or 408A, an individual retirement annuity described in Code section 408(b), a SIMPLE IRA described in Code section 408(p), an annuity plan described in Code section 403(a), an annuity contract described in Code section 403(b), an eligible plan under Code section 457(b) that is maintained by a state, political subdivision of a state, or an agency or instrumentality of a state or political subdivision of a state (and that agrees to separately account for amounts transferred into such plan from this Plan), or a qualified plan described in Code section 401(a) that accepts the Recipient’s Eligible Rollover Distribution. The definition of Eligible Retirement Plan will also apply to the case of a distribution to a surviving Spouse, or to a Spouse or former Spouse who is the Alternate Payee under a Qualified Domestic Relations Order, as defined in Code section 414(p). If any portion of an Eligible Rollover Distribution is attributable to payments or distributions from a designated Roth account, an Eligible Rollover Distribution will include only another designated Roth account of the individual from whose account the payments or distributions were made, or a Roth IRA of such individual. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to eligibility for certain types of contributions or rollovers within retirement plans, but they govern different aspects. The Relius provision specifically addresses In-Plan Roth Rollover Contributions/Transfers and the eligibility of participants, while the Ascensus provision defines what constitutes an Eligible Retirement Plan for Direct Rollover purposes. They do not cover the same plan feature directly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision focuses on eligibility for In-Plan Roth Rollover Contributions and specifies conditions for participants and beneficiaries, whereas the Ascensus provision outlines the types of plans that qualify for rollovers, including definitions related to surviving spouses and alternate payees.

relius_provision_text: Partially Vested accounts. IRRs and IRTs are permitted only from Vested amounts allocated to a qualifying source but may be made from partially Vested Accounts unless otherwise elected in its Adoption Agreement. If a distribution is made to a Participant who has not incurred a Severance from Employment and who is not fully Vested in the Participant's Account from which the In-Plan Roth Rollover Contribution is to be made, and the Participant may increase the Vested percentage in such Account, then at any relevant time Section 6.5(h) will apply to determine the Participant's Vested portion of the Account. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: A. Eligibility – If elected by the Adopting Employer in the Adoption Agreement, Recipients may complete Direct In-Plan Roth Rollovers. Unless otherwise elected in the Adoption Agreement, a Plan that allows Direct In-Plan Roth Rollovers will allow Direct In-Plan Roth Rollovers of all non-Roth contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address In-Plan Roth Rollovers, but they differ in eligibility criteria and the specifics of how contributions can be rolled over. The Relius provision allows for rollovers from partially vested accounts under certain conditions, while the Ascensus provision allows rollovers of all non-Roth contributions if elected, without specifying vesting conditions.
variance_notes: Key differences include Relius's focus on partially vested accounts and the application of vesting percentages, while Ascensus broadly allows rollovers of all non-Roth contributions without such conditions.

relius_provision_text: Salary deferral elections. Each Participant may elect to defer a portion of Compensation which would have been received in the Plan Year, but for the salary deferral election, subject to the limitations of this Section and the Adoption Agreement. A salary deferral election (or modification of an earlier election) may not be made with respect to Compensation which is currently available on or before the date the Participant executed such election, or if later, the later of the date the Employer adopts this cash or deferred arrangement or the date such arrangement first becomes effective. Any elections made pursuant to this Section, including a modification or termination of an election, shall become effective as soon as administratively feasible following the receipt of such election by the Administrator. Furthermore, if the Employer elects in the Adoption Agreement to apply the Automatic Contribution Arrangement provisions, then in the event a Participant fails to make an Affirmative Election, such Participant shall be deemed to have made a salary deferral election in accordance with the provisions selected in the Adoption Agreement and such other procedures that the Administrator may establish and apply in a uniform and nondiscriminatory basis. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Employer elects to take Qualified Matching Contributions into account as Elective Deferrals for purposes of the ADP test, then (subject to such other requirements as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury) the Employer may elect, in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, to either include all Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test or to include only the amount of such Qualified Matching Contributions that are needed to meet the ADP test. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions in retirement plans, but they govern different aspects of the matching contributions. The Relius provision focuses on salary deferral elections and the implications of automatic contributions, while the Ascensus provision discusses Qualified Matching Contributions in relation to the ADP test. The options available in the Adoption Agreements also differ significantly, particularly regarding the specific matching contribution structures and eligibility requirements.
variance_notes: Relius emphasizes salary deferral elections and automatic contributions, while Ascensus focuses on Qualified Matching Contributions for ADP testing. The specific options in the Adoption Agreements are not directly comparable.

relius_provision_text: Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement (EACA). If elected in the Adoption Agreement, the Employer maintains a Plan with Automatic Deferral provisions as an Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement (EACA) and the following provisions will apply: (1) Participants subject to EACA. The Employer in its Adoption Agreement will elect which Participants are subject to the EACA on the 'EACA Effective Date' thereof which may include some or all current Participants or may be limited to those Employees who become Participants after the EACA Effective Date. The 'EACA Effective Date' means the date on which the EACA goes into effect, either as to the Plan or as to an individual Participant as the context requires. An EACA becomes effective as to the Plan as of the date the Employer elects in the Adoption Agreement. A Participant's 'EACA Effective Date' is as soon as practicable after the Participant is subject to Automatic Deferrals under the EACA, consistent with: (i) applicable law; and (ii) the objective of affording the Participant a reasonable period of time after receipt of the EACA notice to make an Affirmative Election (and, if applicable, an investment election). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means an Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement, as described in Code section 414(w) and Plan Section 3.01(E)(2), where Employees are automatically enrolled as Contributing Participants in the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions describe the Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement (EACA) and outline eligibility for automatic enrollment in the plan. They govern the same plan feature, focusing on automatic deferral provisions for participants.
variance_notes: The Relius provision is more detailed regarding the EACA effective date and participant notification, while the Ascensus provision is more concise and references specific sections of the Code. The options in the adoption agreements differ in naming and structure but ultimately serve similar purposes regarding eligibility and contributions.

relius_provision_text: ADP test. Except as otherwise provided herein, this Subsection applies if the prior year testing method is elected in the Adoption Agreement. The "Actual Deferral Percentage" (hereinafter ADP) for a Plan Year for Participants who are Highly Compensated Employees (hereinafter "HCEs") for each Plan Year and the prior year's ADP for Participants who were NHCEs (hereinafter "NHCEs") for the prior Plan Year must satisfy one of the following tests: (1) The ADP for a Plan Year for Participants who are HCEs for the Plan Year shall not exceed the prior year's ADP for Participants who were NHCEs for the prior Plan Year multiplied by 1.25; or (2) The ADP for a Plan Year for Participants who are HCEs for the Plan Year shall not exceed the prior year's ADP for Participants who were NHCEs for the prior Plan Year by more than two (2) percentage points. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, for a specified group of Participants (either Highly Compensated Employees or non-Highly Compensated Employees) for a Plan Year, the average of the ratios (calculated separately for each Participant in such group) of 1) the amount of Employer Contributions actually paid to the Fund on behalf of such Participant for the Plan Year to 2) the Participant’s Compensation for such Plan Year. For purposes of calculating the ADP, Employer Contributions on behalf of any Participant will include: 1) any Elective Deferrals (other than Catch-up Contributions or Elective Deferrals subsequently distributed as a permissible withdrawal) made pursuant to the Participant’s salary deferral election or pursuant to automatic Elective Deferral enrollment, if applicable (including Excess Elective Deferrals of Highly Compensated Employees), but excluding a) Excess Elective Deferrals of Participants who are non-Highly Compensated Employees that arise solely from Elective Deferrals made under the Plan or plans of this Employer and b) Elective Deferrals that are taken into account in the Actual Deferral Percentage test (provided that the ADP test is satisfied both before and after exclusion of these Elective Deferrals); and 2) if elected by the Employer, Qualified Nonelective Contributions and/or Qualified Matching Contributions. For purposes of computing Actual Deferral Percentages, an Employee who would be a Participant but for the failure to make Elective Deferrals will be treated as a Participant on whose behalf no Elective Deferrals are made. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the Actual Deferral Percentage (ADP) test for retirement plans, focusing on the contributions made by Highly Compensated Employees (HCEs) and Non-Highly Compensated Employees (NHCEs). They outline similar requirements for compliance with ADP testing and include options for matching contributions.
variance_notes: The Relius provision specifies the conditions under which the ADP for HCEs must not exceed the prior year's ADP for NHCEs, while the Ascensus provision provides a more detailed calculation method for ADP. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes options for Qualified Nonelective Contributions and Qualified Matching Contributions, which are not explicitly mentioned in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, if the current year testing method is elected in the Adoption Agreement, or if no election is made in the Adoption Agreement, and for any Plan Year for which the Employer has either reserved the right to make a nonselective "ADP test safe harbor contribution" pursuant to Section 12.8 or amended the Plan to make an "ADP test safe harbor contribution," the ADP tests in (a)(1) and (a)(2) above shall be applied by comparing the current Plan Year's ADP for Participants who are HCEs with the current Plan Year's ADP for Participants who are NHCEs for the prior Plan Year, and shall be applied with respect to the NHCEs in the prior Plan Year; provided, however, that if the Plan uses a safe harbor method for the current year test, the ADP for NHCEs who are NHCEs in the current year shall be treated as if they were NHCEs for the current year. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions – Unless such contributions are otherwise limited in the Adoption Agreement, the Employer will make the ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions, if any, indicated in the Adoption Agreement on behalf of each Eligible Employee for the Plan Year.

The Employer may make ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions at the same time as it contributes Elective Deferrals or at any other time as permitted by law and regulation.

If the Employer makes ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions to the Plan based on Compensation earned and Elective Deferrals made during a portion of the Matching Contribution Computation Period (e.g., on a payroll basis when the Matching Contribution Computation Period is Plan Year), the Employer shall re-calculate ("true-up") the ADP Safe Harbor Contributions made to the Plan for that Plan Year. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the ADP test safe harbor contributions for retirement plans, outlining the employer's obligations to make contributions based on employee elective deferrals. They also provide for similar options in the adoption agreements, indicating that the employer can elect to make contributions based on specific criteria.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific language about the current year testing method and comparisons between HCEs and NHCEs, while the Ascensus provision focuses more on the timing and recalculation of contributions. However, both ultimately serve the same purpose of ensuring compliance with ADP testing requirements.

relius_provision_text: If elected in the Adoption Agreement, this Plan is intended to be a SIMPLE 401(k) plan which satisfies the requirements of Code §§401(k)(1) and 401(m)(10).
ascensus_provision_text: Notwithstanding the preceding, the ADP test described above is treated as satisfied for any SIMPLE 401(k) Year in which an Eligible Employer maintains this Plan as a SIMPLE 401(k) Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to SIMPLE 401(k) plans, but they address different aspects. The Relius provision states the plan's intention to be a SIMPLE 401(k) plan, while the Ascensus provision discusses the ADP test satisfaction for SIMPLE 401(k) years. They do not govern the same feature directly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision focuses on the plan's classification, whereas the Ascensus provision deals with compliance testing related to contributions. The options/elections in the Ascensus provision pertain to participant eligibility and allocation, which are not addressed in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: (1) The Employer adopting this Plan is an \"eligible employer.\" An \"eligible employer\" means, with respect to any \"year,\" an Employer that had no more than 100 Employees who received at least $5,000 of \"compensation\" from the Employer for the preceding \"year.\" In applying the preceding sentence, all employees of an Affiliated Employer and Leased Employees are taken into account.\n\nAn \"eligible employer\" that has elected to use the SIMPLE 401(k) provisions but fails to be an \"eligible employer\" for any subsequent \"year,\" is treated as an \"eligible employer\" for the two (2) years following the last \"year\" the Employer was an \"eligible employer.\" If the failure is due to any acquisition, disposition, or similar transaction involving an \"eligible employer,\" the preceding sentence applies only if the provisions of Code §410(b)(6)(C)(1) are satisfied. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, with respect to any SIMPLE 401(k) Year, an Employer that had no more than 100 Employees who received at least $5,000 of Compensation, or such lesser amount indicated in the Adoption Agreement, from the Employer for the preceding SIMPLE 401(k) Year and is therefore eligible to establish a SIMPLE 401(k) Plan. In applying the preceding sentence, all Employees of controlled groups of corporations under Code section 414(b), all Employees of trades or businesses (whether incorporated or not) under Code section 414(c), all Employees of affiliated service groups under Code section 414(m), and Leased Employees required to be treated as the Employers' Employees under Code section 414(n), are taken into account. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define an 'eligible employer' for a SIMPLE 401(k) plan based on the number of employees and compensation criteria. They also account for affiliated employees and leased employees, indicating a similar structure and intent.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific language about the treatment of employers who cease to be eligible due to certain transactions, which is not explicitly mentioned in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Ascensus provision allows for a lesser compensation amount to be indicated in the Adoption Agreement, which is not present in the Relius text.

relius_provision_text: (2) No contributions are made, or benefits accrued for services during the \"year,\" on behalf of any \"eligible employee\" under any other plan, contract, pension, or trust described in Code §219(g)(5)(A) or (B), maintained by the Employer. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will determine the eligibility of each Employee to be a Participant. This determination will be conclusive and binding upon all persons except as otherwise provided herein or by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address eligibility for participation in a retirement plan, but they do so in different ways. The Relius provision specifies conditions under which contributions are not made for eligible employees under certain plans, while the Ascensus provision grants the Plan Administrator the authority to determine eligibility, making it more flexible and administrative in nature.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific exclusions related to contributions and benefits under other plans, while the Ascensus provision focuses on the administrative determination of eligibility without such exclusions. Additionally, the options available in the adoption agreements differ in focus, with Relius emphasizing eligibility conditions and Ascensus allowing for administrative discretion.

relius_provision_text: "Compensation" means, for purposes of this Article, the sum of the wages, tips, and other compensation from the Employer subject to federal income tax withholding (as described in Code §6051(a)(3)) and the Employee's salary deferrals made under this or any other 401(k) plan, and, if applicable, elective deferrals under a Code §408(p) SIMPLE plan, or a Code §403(b) annuity contract and compensation deferred under a Code §457 plan, as described in Code §6051(a)(8). For Self-Employed Individuals, "compensation" means net earnings from self-employment determined under Code §1402(a) prior to subtracting any contributions made under this Plan on behalf of the individual. "Compensation" also includes amounts paid for domestic service (as described in Code §3401(a)(3)). The provisions of the Plan implementing the limit on Compensation under Code §401(a)(17) apply to the "compensation" under this Article. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The annual Compensation of each Participant taken into account in determining allocations will not exceed $200,000, as adjusted for cost-of-living increases in accordance with Code section 414(j)(7)(B). Annual Compensation means Compensation (as defined in Code section 6041(a)(3)) and the Employee's Elective Deferral contributions made under this Plan, and if applicable, elective deferrals under a Code section 408(p) SIMPLE IRA plan, a SARSEP plan, a Code section 403(b) annuity contract, and compensation deferred under a Code section 457 plan, required to be reported by the Employer on Form W-2 (as described in Code section 6041(a)(3)). For Self-Employed Individuals, Compensation means net earnings from self-employment determined under Code section 402(a)(3) before subtracting any contributions made on behalf of the individual. The provisions of the Plan implementing the limit on Compensation under Code section 401(a)(17) apply to the Compensation in Plan Sections 3.01(l) and 3.02. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define 'Compensation' for the purpose of determining matching contributions and include similar components such as wages, tips, elective deferrals, and self-employment earnings. They also reference the same IRS code sections for limits on compensation.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific references to federal income tax withholding and domestic service compensation, while the Ascensus provision focuses on annual compensation limits and cost-of-living adjustments. Additionally, the adoption agreement elections differ in naming conventions and specific options available, but they serve similar purposes in determining matching contributions.

relius_provision_text: "Eligible employee" means, for purposes of this Article, any Participant who is entitled to make Elective Deferrals described in Code §402(g) under the terms of the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will determine the eligibility of each Employee to be a Participant. This determination will be conclusive and binding upon all persons except as otherwise provided herein or by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address eligibility for participation in a retirement plan, but they differ in their approach. The Relius provision defines 'Eligible Employee' specifically in terms of Elective Deferrals under the Code, while the Ascensus provision grants the Plan Administrator broad discretion to determine eligibility, which may not be limited to Elective Deferrals. This fundamental difference in authority and specificity affects the overall interpretation of eligibility.
variance_notes: Relius specifies eligibility based on a defined criterion (Elective Deferrals), while Ascensus allows for a broader administrative determination, which could encompass additional factors beyond those in Relius.

relius_provision_text: (2) The total salary deferral contribution for the \"year\" for any Employee cannot exceed the limitation on salary deferral contributions in effect for the \"year\" pursuant to Code §408(p)(2)(E). The limit will be adjusted by the Secretary of the Treasury for cost-of-living increases under Code §408(p)(2)(E). Any such adjustments will be in multiples of $500. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions made pursuant to the Safe Harbor CODA formula described in Adoption Agreement Section Three, in an amount equal to (i) 100 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that do not exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation for the Plan Year, plus (ii) 50 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation but that do not exceed five-percent of the Employee's Compensation, if applicable. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions, but they describe different matching formulas and eligibility criteria. The Relius provision focuses on salary deferral limits and includes a safe harbor contribution, while the Ascensus provision specifies a tiered matching formula based on employee compensation percentages.
variance_notes: The Relius provision emphasizes compliance with IRS limits and includes a safe harbor contribution, whereas the Ascensus provision outlines a specific matching formula based on employee deferrals and does not mention IRS limits or safe harbor contributions explicitly.

relius_provision_text: In addition to any other election periods provided under the Plan, each "eligible employee" may make or modify a salary deferral election during the 60-day period immediately preceding each January 1st. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: In addition to any other election periods provided under the Plan, each Eligible Employee may make or modify a deferral election during the 30-day period immediately following receipt of the notice described above. Notwithstanding the preceding, the Employer may change the election periods described above pursuant to rules promulgated by the IRS. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern election periods for salary deferral elections, but they differ in the duration of the election period (60 days vs. 30 days) and the context in which they apply (one is specific to January 1st, while the other is based on receipt of a notice).
variance_notes: The Relius provision allows for a 60-day election period before January 1st, while the Ascensus provision allows for a 30-day period following a notice. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes a clause allowing the employer to change election periods, which is not present in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: For the "year" an Employee becomes eligible to make salary deferral contributions under this Article, the 60-day election period requirement of Subsection (a)(1) is deemed satisfied if the Employee may make or modify a salary deferral election during a 60-day period that includes either the date the Employee becomes eligible or the day before. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: In addition to any other election periods provided under the Plan, each Eligible Employee may make or modify a deferral election during the 30-day period immediately following receipt of the notice described above. Notwithstanding the preceding, the Employer may change the election periods described above pursuant to rules promulgated by the IRS. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to eligibility and election periods for employee contributions, but they govern different aspects: the Relius provision focuses on salary deferral contributions and a 60-day election period, while the Ascensus provision addresses a 30-day election period for deferral elections and includes a matching contribution context. The differences in time frames and the specific focus on matching contributions in the Ascensus provision indicate they are not semantically equivalent.
variance_notes: Key differences include the election period duration (60 days vs. 30 days) and the specific focus on salary deferral contributions in Relius versus deferral elections and matching contributions in Ascensus.

relius_provision_text: Each "eligible employee" may terminate a salary deferral election at any time during the "year". [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will determine the eligibility of each Employee to be a Participant. This determination will be conclusive and binding upon all persons except as otherwise provided herein or by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to eligibility for participation in a retirement plan, but they govern different aspects. The Relius provision specifically addresses the ability of eligible employees to terminate salary deferral elections, while the Ascensus provision focuses on the Plan Administrator's authority to determine eligibility, which is a broader administrative function. The options available in the adoption agreements also differ in focus, with Relius emphasizing eligibility conditions and Ascensus detailing administrative determinations.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Relius provision's focus on employee actions regarding salary deferral elections, while the Ascensus provision emphasizes the administrative determination of eligibility, which may not directly correlate with employee actions.

relius_provision_text: The Employer will notify each "eligible employee" prior to the 60-day election period described in Section 13.4(a) that a salary deferral election or a modification to a prior election may be made during that period. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: In addition to any other election periods provided under the Plan, each Eligible Employee may make or modify a deferral election during the 30-day period immediately following receipt of the notice described above. Notwithstanding the preceding, the Employer may change the election periods described above pursuant to rules promulgated by the IRS. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employee eligibility for making salary deferral elections, but they differ in the specifics of the election periods and the conditions under which employees can modify their elections. The Relius provision specifies a 60-day election period, while the Ascensus provision specifies a 30-day period following receipt of notice. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes a clause allowing the employer to change election periods, which is not present in the Relius provision.
variance_notes: The Relius provision focuses on notifying eligible employees about a 60-day election period, while the Ascensus provision allows for a 30-day modification period after notice and includes flexibility for the employer to change election periods.

relius_provision_text: The notification described in (1) above will indicate whether the Employer will provide a matching contribution described in Section 13.3(b)(1) or a two percent (2%) Nonelective Contribution described in Section 13.3(b)(2) for that "year." [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions made pursuant to the Safe Harbor CODA formula described in Adoption Agreement Section Three, in an amount equal to (i) 100 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that do not exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation for the Plan Year, plus (ii) 50 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation but that do not exceed five-percent of the Employee's Compensation, if applicable. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions, but they differ in structure and specific requirements. The Relius provision allows for a choice between a traditional matching contribution and a Nonelective Contribution, while the Ascensus provision specifies a Safe Harbor matching formula with defined percentages. The options available in the adoption agreements also differ significantly.
variance_notes: Relius allows for a 2% Nonelective Contribution option, which is not present in Ascensus. Additionally, Ascensus has a tiered matching structure based on employee deferrals, which is not specified in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: Claims for benefits under the Plan may be filed in writing with the Administrator. Written or electronic notice of the disposition of a claim shall be furnished to the claimant within ninety (90) days (45 days if the claim involves disability benefits and disability is not based on the Social Security Acts) after the application is filed, or such period as required by applicable law or Department of Labor regulation. Any electronic notification shall comply with the standards imposed by Department of Labor Regulation §2520.104b-1(c)(1)(i), (iii) and (iv) or any subsequent guidance. In the event the claim is denied, the reasons for the denial shall be specifically set forth in the notice in language calculated to be understood by the claimant, pertinent provisions of the Plan shall be cited, and, where appropriate, an explanation as to how the claimant can perfect the claim will be provided. In addition, the claimant shall be furnished with an explanation of the Plan's claims review procedure.
ascensus_provision_text: Remedies Available — The Participant or Beneficiary will have 60 days from receipt of the denial notice in which to make written application for review by the Plan Administrator. The Participant or Beneficiary may request that the review be in the nature of a hearing. The Participant or Beneficiary will have the right to representation, to review pertinent documents, and to submit comments in writing (or in any other form permitted by the IRS or DOL). The Plan Administrator shall issue a decision on such review within 60 days after receipt of an application for review as provided for in this Plan Section 5.09 and pursuant to Department of Labor regulation Section 2560.503-1. If the claim involves disability benefits under the Plan, the Participant or Beneficiary will have 180 days from receipt of the denial notice in which to make written application for review by the Plan Administrator. The Plan Administrator shall issue a decision on such review within 45 days after receipt of an application for review as provided for in this Plan Section 5.09 and pursuant to Labor regulation Section 2560.503-1. Upon a decision unfavorable to the Participant or Beneficiary, such Participant or Beneficiary will be entitled to bring such actions in law or equity as may be necessary or appropriate to protect or clarify their right to benefits under this Plan. The Participant or Beneficiary will have one year from receipt of the denial notice to bring such action.
reasoning: Both provisions relate to claims for benefits under a retirement plan, but they govern different aspects of the claims process. The Relius provision focuses on the initial claim filing and the timeline for notification of the claim's disposition, while the Ascensus provision addresses the review process after a claim has been denied, including timelines for appeals and rights of the participant or beneficiary.
variance_notes: Key differences include the focus on initial claims in the Relius provision versus the appeals process in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the timelines for responses differ, with Relius specifying 90 days (or 45 for disability) for initial claims, while Ascensus specifies 60 days for standard appeals and 45 days for disability appeals.

relius_provision_text: Any Employee, Former Employee, or Beneficiary of either, who has been denied a benefit by a decision of the Administrator pursuant to Section 2.10 shall be entitled to request the Administrator to give further consideration to the claim by filing with the Administrator a written request. Such request, together with a written statement of the reasons why the claimant believes such claim should be allowed, shall be filed with the Administrator no later than sixty (60) days after receipt of the written notification provided for in Section 2.10(a). A final decision as to the allowance of the claim shall be made by the Administrator within sixty (60) days (45 days if the claim involves disability benefits and disability is not based on the Social Security Acts) of receipt of the appeal (unless there has been an extension of sixty (60) days (45 days if the claim involves disability benefits and disability is not based on the Social Security Acts) due to special circumstances, provided the delay and the special circumstances occasioned are communicated to the claimant within the sixty (60) day period (45 days if the claim involves disability benefits and disability is not based on the Social Security Acts). Such communication shall be written in a manner calculated to be understood by the claimant and shall include specific reasons for the decision and specific references to the pertinent Plan provisions on which the decision is based. The communication may be written or electronic (provided the electronic communication complies with the standards imposed by Department of Labor Regulation §2520.104b-1(c)(1)(i), (iii) and (iv) or any subsequent guidance). Notwithstanding the preceding, to the extent any of the time periods specified in this Section are amended by law or Department of Labor regulation, then the time frames specified herein shall automatically be changed in accordance with such law or regulation.
ascensus_provision_text: Remedies Available — The Participant or Beneficiary will have 60 days from receipt of the denial notice in which to make written application for review by the Plan Administrator. The Participant or Beneficiary may request that the review be in the nature of a hearing. The Participant or Beneficiary will have the right to representation, to review pertinent documents, and to submit comments in writing (or in any other form permitted by the IRS or DOL). The Plan Administrator shall issue a decision on such review within 60 days after receipt of an application for review as provided for in this Plan Section 5.09 and pursuant to Department of Labor regulation Section 2560.503-1. If the claim involves disability benefits under the Plan, the Participant or Beneficiary will have 180 days from receipt of the denial notice in which to make written application for review by the Plan Administrator. The Plan Administrator shall issue a decision on such review within 45 days after receipt of an application for review as provided for in this Plan Section 5.09 and pursuant to Labor regulation Section 2560.503-1. Upon a decision unfavorable to the Participant or Beneficiary, such Participant or Beneficiary will be entitled to bring such actions in law or equity as may be necessary or appropriate to protect or clarify their right to benefits under this Plan. The Participant or Beneficiary will have one year from receipt of the denial notice to bring such action.
reasoning: Both provisions govern the claims review process for denied benefits, outlining the rights of participants or beneficiaries to appeal decisions made by the plan administrator. They specify time frames for filing appeals and for the administrator's response, as well as the rights of participants during the review process.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision allows for a hearing and representation, which is not explicitly mentioned in the Relius provision. Additionally, the time frame for filing an appeal for disability benefits differs slightly, with Relius allowing 45 days for the administrator's decision, while Ascensus allows 45 days for the review decision but extends the appeal period to 180 days for disability claims.

relius_provision_text: General rule. An Eligible Employee who has satisfied the conditions of eligibility pursuant to Section 3.1 shall become a Participant effective as of the date elected in the Adoption Agreement. Regardless of any election in the Adoption Agreement to the contrary, an Eligible Employee who has satisfied the maximum age (21) and service requirements (one (1) Year or (Period) of Service (or, with respect to contributions other than Employee Deferrals, more than one (1) year if full administrative vesting) and who is otherwise entitled to participate, will become a Participant no later than the earlier of (1) six (6) months after such requirements are satisfied, or (2) the first day of the first Plan Year after such requirements are satisfied, unless the Employee separates from service before such participation date. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: The Plan Administrator will determine the eligibility of each Employee to be a Participant. This determination will be conclusive and binding upon all persons except as otherwise provided herein or by law. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address eligibility for participation in a retirement plan, but they do so in different ways.
